description_long: This is a pixelated, military-style airplane with a dark green and brown camouflage pattern, featuring a broad fuselage and straight wings. The wings are mounted mid-fuselage and each supports a large, circular engine nacelle, suggesting a twin-engine configuration. The tail section is robust, with a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a slightly raised canopy. The landing gear is visible as two main struts beneath the wings and a nose gear, all rendered in a reddish color. The overall design is utilitarian, with blocky, angular features typical of voxel art.
description: A blocky, camouflaged twin-engine airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and visible landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin engine nacelles on the wings, the central cockpit canopy, and the broad wingspan.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the engine nacelle profile, and the extended landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal stabilizers, and the two main landing gear struts.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the engine nacelle and landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed and non-retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single-seat.
